paper.title: Systematic search for blue hyper-velocity stars from LAMOST survey

extraction.summary: Extracted 10 new unbound HVS candidates (LAMOST-HVS5 through LAMOST-HVS14) explicitly identified by the paper as exceeding the local Milky Way escape velocity.

method_chain.summary: LAMOST DR10 low-resolution spectra and Gaia DR3 astrometry/photometry

LAMOST-HVS5 discussion: unbound A0III star with v_tot=806 km/s

inclusion_assessment.summary: Paper explicitly identifies LAMOST-HVS5 as a new unbound hypervelocity star candidate with total velocity 806.1+/-276.6 km/s exceeding the local Milky Way escape velocity.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper explicitly identifies this object as a new unbound HVS candidate with total velocity exceeding the local escape velocity.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The remaining high-velocity star candidates from the 125-object catalog that are not explicitly identified as new unbound HVS candidates.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents these objects as high-velocity star candidates but does not explicitly identify them as unbound from the Milky Way. Only ten candidates (LAMOST-HVS5-14) are explicitly labeled as new unbound HVSs.

source_refs.context: Table tab-hivel: 125 high-velocity candidates
Final sample of 125 high-velocity candidates with velocity criteria

candidate_groups_considered.description: SDSS J081828.07+570922.1, recovered in the sample and explicitly identified as a bound HVS.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly identifies this object as a bound HVS discovered by Brown et al. 2007, not as an unbound candidate.

source_refs.context: Known HVSs recovered in the sample, with bound HVS explicitly noted

candidate_groups_considered.description: HIP 60350, recovered in the sample and identified as a hyper-runaway star.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper identifies this object as a hyper-runaway star, not as a Galactic-unbound HVS candidate.

source_refs.context: Known hyper-runaway star recovered in the sample

candidate_groups_considered.description: J234921.86+090714.5, the only white dwarf in the sample, discussed as a possible halo member.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper discusses this white dwarf as a possible extreme halo member with a total velocity below 600 km/s, not as an unbound HVS candidate.

source_refs.context: Special white dwarf discussion

candidate_groups_considered.description: J153747.37+034101.2, a type-c RR Lyrae star with high radial velocity discussed in the special stars section.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper discusses this RR Lyrae star as a high-velocity star but does not present it as an unbound HVS candidate.

source_refs.context: RR Lyrae star discussion
